Schwab-Client-CorrelId.description: Used to identify an individual request throughout the lifetime of the request and across systems.

SchwabClientCorrelId.description: This **Correlation ID** is unique to the operation. The generated GUID can be used to track an individual service call if support is needed.

QueryParamDate.description: Valid date range is from currentdate to 1 year from today. It will default to current day if not entered. Date format:YYYY-MM-DD
QueryParamDate.name: date

QueryParamDaysToExpiration.description: Days to expiration to use in calculations. Applies only to ANALYTICAL strategy chains (see strategy param)
QueryParamDaysToExpiration.name: daysToExpiration

QueryParamEntitlement.description: Applicable only if its retail token, entitlement of client PP-PayingPro, NP-NonPro and PN-NonPayingPro
QueryParamEntitlement.name: entitlement

QueryParamFrequency.description: To return movers with the specified directions of up or down
QueryParamFrequency.name: frequency

QueryParamInterestRate.description: Interest rate to use in calculations. Applies only to ANALYTICAL strategy chains (see strategy param)
QueryParamInterestRate.name: interestRate

QueryParamInterval.description: Strike interval for spread strategy chains (see strategy param)
QueryParamInterval.name: interval

QueryParamIsRealTimeRequest.description: Only applicable if its Advisor token, Get realtime Optionchain and skip entitlement check
QueryParamIsRealTimeRequest.name: isRealTimeRequest

QueryParamStrategy.description: OptionChain strategy. Default is SINGLE. ANALYTICAL allows the use of volatility, underlyingPrice, interestRate, and daysToExpiration params to calculate theoretical values.
QueryParamStrategy.name: strategy

QueryParamStrikeCount.description: The Number of strikes to return above or below the at-the-money price
QueryParamStrikeCount.name: strikeCount

QueryParamUnderlyingPrice.description: Underlying price to use in calculations. Applies only to ANALYTICAL strategy chains (see strategy param)
QueryParamUnderlyingPrice.name: underlyingPrice

QueryParamVolatility.description: Volatility to use in calculations.  Applies only to ANALYTICAL strategy chains (see strategy param)
QueryParamVolatility.name: volatility

QueryParamenddate.description:
The end date, Time in milliseconds since the UNIX epoch eg 1451624400000
If not specified, the endDate will default to the market close of previous business day.
QueryParamenddate.name: endDate

QueryParamfrequency.description:
The time frequency duration

If the frequencyType is
• minute - valid values are 1, 5, 10, 15, 30
• daily - valid value is 1
• weekly - valid value is 1
• monthly - valid value is 1

If frequency is not specified, default value is 1
QueryParamfrequency.name: frequency

QueryParamfrequencytype.description:
The time frequencyType

If the periodType is
• day - valid value is minute
• month - valid values are daily, weekly
• year - valid values are daily, weekly, monthly
• ytd - valid values are daily, weekly

If frequencyType is not specified, default value depends on the periodType
• day - defaulted to minute.
• month - defaulted to weekly.
• year - defaulted to monthly.
• ytd - defaulted to weekly.
QueryParamfrequencytype.name: frequencyType

QueryParamperiod.description:
The number of chart period types.

If the periodType is
• day - valid values are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 10
• month - valid values are 1, 2, 3, 6
• year - valid values are 1, 2, 3, 5, 10, 15, 20
• ytd - valid values are 1

If the period is not specified and the periodType is
• day - default period is 10.
• month - default period is 1.
• year - default period is 1.
• ytd - default period is 1.
QueryParamperiod.name: period

QueryParamstartdate.description:
The start date, Time in milliseconds since the UNIX epoch eg 1451624400000
If not specified startDate will be (endDate - period) excluding weekends and holidays.
QueryParamstartdate.name: startDate

QueryParamsymbol.description: The Equity symbol used to look up price history
QueryParamsymbol.name: symbol

header-apmid.description: APM ID to identify client uniqueid
header-apmid.name: Schwab-Client-AppId

header-correlid.description: This **Correlation ID** is unique to the operation. The GUID that is generated can be used to track an individual service call if support is needed.

header-functionid.description: Identifies the page or source in the channel where quote is being called from. MDS will provide you the Function ID’s to use after getting a walk through of the pages needing MDS Quote data.
header-functionid.name: Schwab-Client-FunctionId

quotes-query-param-cusips.description: Comma separated list of cusip(s) to look up a quote
quotes-query-param-cusips.name: cusips

quotes-query-param-fields.description: Request for subset of data by passing coma separated list of root nodes, possible root nodes are quote, fundamental, extended, reference, regular. Sending `quote, fundamental` in request will return quote and fundamental data in response. Dont send this attribute for full response.
quotes-query-param-fields.name: fields

quotes-query-param-indicative.description: Include indicative symbol quotes for all ETF symbols in request. If ETF symbol ABC is in request and indicative=true API will return quotes for ABC and its corresponding indicative quote for $ABC.IV
quotes-query-param-indicative.name: indicative

quotes-query-param-realtime.description: Only applicable if its Advisor token, returns realtime quotes and skip entitlement check
quotes-query-param-realtime.name: realtime

quotes-query-param-ssids.description: Comma separated list of Schwab Unique Security Indetifier SSID(s) to look up a quote
quotes-query-param-ssids.name: ssids

quotes-query-param-symbols.description: Comma separated list of symbol(s) to look up a quote
quotes-query-param-symbols.name: symbols

ExpirationType.description: M for End Of Month Expiration Calendar Cycle. (To match the last business day of the month), Q for Quarterly expirations (last business day of the quarter month MAR/JUN/SEP/DEC), W for Weekly expiration (also called Friday Short Term Expirations) and S for Expires 3rd Friday of the month (also known as regular options).
